_doc: Lattice + space group reference data for tools/twin-law-check.mjs.
Each entry is a single crystallographic refinement: unit-cell parameters
(Å for lengths, ° for angles), space-group symbol in Hermann-Mauguin
form, and the literature citation the values came from. The tool reads
this file, computes structural twin-plane candidates from the lattice
geometry, and compares them against declared twin_laws in
Initial population (this commit): 17 minerals — the canonical cubic
spinel-law set, the aragonite-group pseudo-hex carbonates, plus a
monoclinic / tetragonal / trigonal / orthorhombic sample. Adamite is
included so the v139 {101} back-test runs automatically. Spinel,
staurolite, gypsum, orthoclase are included even though they don't
have twin_laws entries in minerals.json yet — they're known structural
reference points and the tool will skip them gracefully.
Future populations should add: the remaining 142 minerals-with-twins.
Strategy: do them in batches matching the v137-v141 batch boundaries
(sulfides, phosphates, arsenates, sulfates, others). Each lattice value
must cite a real source. See v142 citation-conservatism rule.

spinel._source: Wikipedia spinel; matches Bosi et al. 2019 (Mineralogical Magazine 83:687-704) structural refinement

magnetite._source: Fleet 1981 (Acta Cryst. B37:917-920) Fe3O4 refinement at 298 K

fluorite._source: standard CaF2 refinement; Anthony Handbook v.III fluorite entry

galena._source: Noda et al. 1987 PbS refinement; Anthony Handbook v.I galena entry

aragonite._source: De Villiers 1971 (Amer. Mineral. 56:758-767) aragonite at 25 °C; Wikipedia aragonite

cerussite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) orthorhombic ACO3 isostructural series

witherite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) BaCO3 entry in the same series

strontianite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) SrCO3 entry in the same series

marcasite._source: Rieder et al. 2007 (Canadian Mineralogist 45:1395-1407) marcasite refinement

adamite._source: Hill 1976 (Amer. Mineral. 61:979-986) adamite structure; matches crystalsymmetry.wordpress.com and Anthony Handbook v.IV

cassiterite._source: Bolzan et al. 1997 (Acta Cryst. B53:373-380) rutile-type oxide refinement series

calcite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V calcite entry; hex setting

quartz._source: Le Page & Donnay 1976 (Acta Cryst. B32:2456-2459) α-quartz refinement

gypsum._source: Cole & Lancucki 1974 (Acta Cryst. B30:921-929) CaSO4·2H2O refinement

orthoclase._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.II orthoclase entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); representative high-Or feldspar values

tremolite._source: Hawthorne 1981 (Reviews in Mineralogy v.9A, amphibole crystal chemistry); Anthony Handbook v.II tremolite entry

actinolite._source: Tremolite-actinolite solid-solution series (Hawthorne 1981 + 2007); slight cell expansion vs. tremolite from Fe²⁺-for-Mg substitution; Anthony Handbook v.II actinolite entry

halite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III halite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); canonical NaCl rock-salt structure, standard reference

sylvite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III sylvite entry; KCl, rock-salt structure isotype with halite

atacamite._source: Parise & Hyde 1986 (Acta Cryst. C42:1277-1280) atacamite refinement; Anthony Handbook v.III atacamite entry

borax._source: Morimoto 1956, The Crystal Structure of Borax (Min. J. Japan 2:1-18); Anthony Handbook v.V borax entry

lepidocrocite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III lepidocrocite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); γ-FeOOH layered structure

native_copper._source: Anthony Handbook v.I native copper entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); standard FCC metal structure

native_gold._source: Anthony Handbook v.I gold entry; standard FCC metal structure

native_silver._source: Anthony Handbook v.I silver entry; standard FCC metal structure

native_bismuth._source: Anthony Handbook v.I bismuth entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); A7 rhombohedral arsenic-group metal structure (shared with native arsenic + antimony)

native_sulfur._source: Anthony Handbook v.I sulfur entry; α-S8 ring molecular structure (Coppens et al. 1977 Acta Cryst. B33:1330-1335)

native_tellurium._source: Anthony Handbook v.I tellurium entry; helical chain structure (isotype with selenium; chiral)

dolomite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V dolomite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); ordered Ca-Mg layered calcite-derivative (R-3 vs calcite R-3c — symmetry broken by cation order)

siderite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V siderite entry; FeCO3, calcite-isostructural

rhodochrosite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V rhodochrosite entry; MnCO3, calcite-isostructural

smithsonite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V smithsonite entry; ZnCO3, calcite-isostructural

malachite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V malachite entry; Susse 1967 (Acta Cryst. 22:146-151) Cu2(CO3)(OH)2 refinement

azurite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V azurite entry; Cu3(CO3)2(OH)2 refinement (Zigan & Schuster 1972 Z. Krist. 135:416-436)

rosasite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V rosasite entry; (Cu,Zn)2(CO3)(OH)2, malachite-isostructural

aurichalcite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V aurichalcite entry; (Zn,Cu)5(CO3)2(OH)6 (Harding et al. 1994 Acta Cryst. C50:852-854)

wulfenite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V wulfenite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); PbMoO4 scheelite-type structure

scheelite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V scheelite entry; CaWO4, type for the scheelite structure family

powellite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V powellite entry; CaMoO4, scheelite-isostructural

stolzite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V stolzite entry; PbWO4 tetragonal polymorph (scheelite-class); dimorphic with raspite

raspite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V raspite entry; PbWO4 monoclinic polymorph; dimorphic with stolzite (Fujita et al. 1977 Acta Cryst. B33:162-168)

wolframite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V wolframite entry; (Fe,Mn)WO4 — solid-solution series between hübnerite (MnWO4) and ferberite (FeWO4)

ferrimolybdite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V ferrimolybdite entry; Fe2(MoO4)3·nH2O hydrated ferric molybdate (data sparse; values approximate; entry may need refinement when better refinement is available)
